steps.title: Enter the first two years
steps.content: A Desmos exponential regression makes the percentage-growth structure visible. At $x=0$, the house is worth $174{,}000$. After one year, it is multiplied by $1+0.024=1.024$, so the value is $174{,}000(1.024)=178{,}176$.

steps.title: Fit the exponential model
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$ in Desmos. The regression gives $a=174000$ and $b=1.024$, so the model is $y=174000(1.024)^x$.

steps.title: Choose the matching function
steps.content: Choice A uses $0.024$ as the whole multiplier, which would make the value shrink. Choice B uses $0.24$, also a decrease. Choice D uses $1.24$, which would be a $24\%$ increase. Choice C uses the regression multiplier $1.024$, so choice C is correct.
